title: with_max_entries(0) is a public OOM footgun documented as 'tests only'
description: Scenario: a user calls CachingStore::with_max_entries(inner, ttl, 0) thinking 0 means 'no limit' (reasonable default assumption for capacity parameters). evict_if_full early-returns on max_entries==0, so the HashMap grows without bound. In a service that sees many distinct secret paths, this is an unbounded memory leak. The comment 'use only in tests' is invisible to someone reading just the signature. Maintenance consequence: OOM bug report from a user with a large cache. Fix: either panic on 0 ('0 is not a valid limit; use usize::MAX for unbounded'), or document usize::MAX as the idiomatic unbounded value and remove the 0 special case, or add a \#\[doc(hidden)\] test-only constructor.
